title: Penne all'Arrabbiata

subs.name: rigatoni, fusilli or any ridged pasta
subs.notes: smooth pasta like linguine won't hold the sauce as well

ingredients.name: ripe tomatoes, roughly chopped, or canned crushed tomatoes

subs.name: one 400 g tin of whole plum tomatoes, crushed by hand
subs.notes: canned tomatoes give a consistent result all year round

ingredients.name: dried red chillies
visual_checks: use 2 for medium heat, 6 for very hot
ingredients.notes: use 2 for medium heat, 6 for very hot

subs.name: 2 tsp red chilli flakes or 3 fresh red chillies, slit
subs.notes: adjust quantity to match your heat tolerance

subs.name: sunflower oil plus a pinch of dried oregano
subs.notes: oregano compensates for the milder oil

steps.text: Bring a large pot of heavily salted water to a rolling boil. Add the penne and cook until al dente according to packet instructions, usually 10–12 minutes. Reserve 200 ml pasta water before draining.

While pasta cooks, warm the olive oil in a wide pan over medium heat. Add the garlic and dried chillies and fry for 1–2 minutes, stirring, until the garlic is pale gold and the chillies darken slightly and release their heat into the oil.

Add the tomatoes with a good pinch of salt. Raise the heat to medium-high and cook uncovered, crushing the tomatoes with the back of a spoon, for 12 minutes until the sauce thickens and oil separates on top.
Add the drained penne to the sauce and toss vigorously over medium heat, adding pasta water a splash at a time until the sauce is glossy and coats each tube — about 2 minutes.
Remove from heat, scatter over the parsley if using, and serve immediately. Do not add cheese — the authentic Roman arrabbiata is cheese-free.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: pasta → rigatoni, fusilli or any ridged pasta (smooth pasta like linguine won't hold the sauce as well); dried-red-chilli → 2 tsp red chilli flakes or 3 fresh red chillies, slit (adjust quantity to match your heat tolerance); tomato → one 400 g tin of whole plum tomatoes, crushed by hand (canned tomatoes give a consistent result all year round); olive-oil → sunflower oil plus a pinch of dried oregano (oregano compensates for the milder oil)
indian_kitchen_adaptation: Cook the sauce in a kadai on medium flame — the wide base helps the tomatoes reduce quickly. Indian dried Kashmiri or byadgi chillies give good colour and moderate heat; add one or two regular dried red chillies for fire. Any penne available in the pasta aisle at supermarkets works; rinse and check cooking times on the packet.
